texas_v10_rule_basis: Preserve noindex until all critical families are verified
District-grade routing must use direct local or district-owned evidence, not generic or statewide fallback
Legacy/inventory-only evidence does not satisfy California-grade completeness

final_blockers.evidence: Reviewed 2026-06-23 one more bounded official Utah county-local pass on the same live public DWS office API. The official `GET https://officesearch-api.jobs.utah.gov/api/v1/offices` payload still returns 99 public rows covering 45 unique offices with office names, addresses, service codes, lat/lng, and assistance instructions, but still no county field, counties-served field, or reusable county-to-office contract. A bounded reverse-geocode audit of those exact official office coordinates still materializes physical offices in only 26 of Utah's 29 counties, leaving Daggett, Morgan, and Rich without even an in-county office point. One more payload-text audit now tightens that blocker further: `Daggett` and `Morgan` do not appear anywhere in the public JSON at all, `Rich` appears only inside `Richfield` office names rather than as Rich County routing, and the only literal `county` office-name tokens are `Emery County (Castle Dale)` and `South County (Taylorsville)`, which is far short of a statewide county contract.
